commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

bump (static r-estimates replace confidence head in v3), Q2 raw_score
confirmed present in dimensions[] — independently verified via direct
curl to endpoint. B2 dead zone visible: HI score 6.69 (dead zone) vs
raw_score 7.46 (differentiated) on test text.

TC sequencing caution adopted — A/B test required before implementing
visitor journey reordering. blog PR #7 merged (cogarch 15 triggers,
Opus 4.6 model correction, interpretant collapse reviewed).

anchor), requesting dimensions[].raw_score on all.

commit.message:
fix(graph): enforce privacy levels on query path (fixes #2)

QueryEngine.assembleContext() now filters entities by project privacy
level before passing context to LLM:
- restricted: excluded entirely from query context
- sensitive: content redacted to '[REDACTED]', properties stripped
- standard: unchanged

This is a single enforcement point that covers all three query entry
points (REST API, CLI, MCP). Previously, privacy was only checked
during ingestion — entities from restricted projects could be queried
out via the API.

Closes #2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: adiciona invalidacao de cache e router.refresh no fluxo de criacao ideias -> agenda

Remove o guard fetchedForRef de useClientData e useUserData.

O guard foi adicionado para evitar double-fetch do StrictMode em dev, mas
causava um bug grave no fluxo Ideias→Agenda: ao montar o componente apos
troca de aba, o StrictMode cancelava o Effect 1 (cancelled=true) enquanto
ele aguardava o pendingSaves, e o Effect 2 encontrava fetchedForRef ja
setado e retornava sem buscar dados — resultado: Agenda sempre vazia.

Com RATE_LIMIT_MAX ja em 200 req/min, o double-fetch do StrictMode (22
hooks x2 = ~44 chamadas) nao causa mais 429. O guard e desnecessario
e prejudicial — removido de ambos os hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Accept `PathLike` in `use_cassette` and `Cassette` (#13)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch to patient, low-frequency trading strategy

- AI now defaults to HOLD most days
- Only recommends ACT for broken thesis, persistent underperformance,
  or clearly superior opportunities
- Thinks in weeks/months, not daily noise
- Targets 5-10 trades per year, not per week
- Reverted to single daily cron (9:35 AM ET) for free tier

commit.message:
fix: Route session exit alerts to #ops and suppress fork respawn noise [Midtown !2123] (#1850)

* fix: Route session exit alerts to #ops and suppress fork respawn noise

Two changes to reduce noise in the main channel:

1. Skip the generic "session exited unexpectedly" message for forks
   that are being respawned. The RespawnFork effect already posts a
   specific "Fork died — respawning" message to #ops, so the generic
   message was redundant.

2. Route coworker/channel-lead exit messages to #ops instead of the
   main channel. Lead exits remain in the main channel since the user
   needs to see those. This follows the existing pattern where other
   health alerts already post to #ops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Include stderr in fork respawn message to preserve crash context

When a fork is being respawned, the generic exit message (which includes
stderr) was suppressed. If the respawn then failed, the crash context
was lost. Now the stderr is included in the "Fork died — respawning"
message posted to #ops, ensuring crash details are always available.

Addresses review feedback from chatgpt-codex-connector on PR #1850.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Document session exit alert routing in architecture.md

Adds a paragraph to the Forked Sessions section describing how session
exit alerts are routed by session type: lead → main channel,
coworker/channel-lead → #ops, fork respawn → #ops with stderr.

Addresses broadway's review feedback on PR #1850.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix scrape: call Railway backend directly, bypass Vercel proxy timeout

the scrape proxy before it completes. Now calls NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
directly for scraping, with 3min timeout per city.

Fix corrupted LaTeX in Expert Systems passage

Python string literal escape sequences (\t, \b, \f, \n) mangled four

\frac → FF+"rac", \neg → newline+"eg". Restored correct LaTeX.

commit.message:
fix: L2 key/query normalization for d-scaling stability (#177)

* fix: add L2 key/query normalization per Titans paper §Architectural Details

Root cause of d=1024 NaN at step 1338: without normalization, memory
update outer products scale as O(d), making M-norm diverge. The Titans
paper explicitly requires ℓ₂-norm on keys and queries before memory ops.

Forward: k_norm = k_raw / max(‖k_raw‖, ε) applied after projection+conv1d.
Backward: Jacobian (I - k̂k̂ᵀ)/max(‖k‖, ε) chains through tape and GPU paths.

Changes across all three tiers:
- CUDA: l2_normalize.cu with forward/backward kernels (warp reduction)
- Rust CPU: tensor.rs helpers, titans_lmm.rs + delta_rule.rs forward/backward
- GPU: gpu_forward.rs (all variants + TNT shards + scratch path),
  gpu_backward.rs (accumulate_projection_grads + TNT Step 5b),
  cuda_graph.rs (GpuLevelScratch norms buffers)
- Wengert tape: opaque_adapters.rs saves/restores norms with backward compat
- GpuBuf::dup() for deep-copy in TNT shard caches
- Spec: 07_l2_key_query_normalization.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings for L2 normalization PR

- Fix CUDA warp mask: use __activemask() instead of hardcoded 0xffffffff
- Fix clamped-branch backward: when norm <= eps, use d_out/eps (linear
  scaling) instead of sphere Jacobian (both CUDA and CPU paths)
- Fix missing q_mem parameter in accumulate_projection_grads
- Fix stale backward-compat comment in opaque_adapters.rs
- Fix spec markdown: backtick math terms, add language tags to fenced blocks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: address nitpick review findings for L2 normalization

- Remove power-of-two block_size rounding in l2_normalize.cu launchers;
  use min(d, 1024) directly so d=768/896 don't waste threads. Reduction
  code (__activemask + cross-warp smem) already handles non-power-of-two.
- Eliminate redundant rematch in TNT backward (gpu_backward.rs): extract
  k_norms/q_norms/k_mem/q_mem before the inner backward match so Step 5b
  uses the already-bound variables directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use >= eps in L2 backward branch condition

When norm == eps, forward computes fmaxf(norm, eps) = norm, so the
sphere Jacobian is correct. The > eps check incorrectly routed this
case to the linear d_out/eps branch. Fixed in both CUDA and CPU paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
refactor(schema): change Field.name from String to Arc<str> (#6351)

Field is cloned frequently during query planning and schema operations.
Each clone previously heap-allocated a new `String` for the name, but
field names are never mutated after construction. `Arc<str>` makes
clones O(1) (atomic refcount bump, no allocation) and shrinks Field by 8
bytes (2 words vs 3).

Core changes:
- `Field.name: String` -> `Arc<str>`
- `Field::new/rename bounds: Into<String>` -> `Into<Arc<str>>`
- `Schema.name_to_indices: HashMap<String, _>` -> `HashMap<Arc<str>, _>`

Downstream fixes across 60 files follow mechanical patterns:
- `.name.as_str()` -> `.name.as_ref()`
- `.name.clone()` where `String` needed -> `.name.to_string()`
- `Field::new(&string_ref, ..)` -> `Field::new(s.as_str(), ..)`
- `HashSet<String>.contains(&f.name)` -> .`contains(&*f.name)`
- `assert_eq!(field.name, "lit")` -> `assert_eq!(&*field.name, "lit")`

PyO3 boundary still returns `String` to Python. Serde, Display, Hash,
and PartialEq semantics are unchanged (all compare str content).

Closes #2892.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
